stories.title: Deadlock over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz cripples peace efforts
stories.description: Two months after the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran started the war, peace talks are on hold, with control of the Strait of Hormuz and the future of Iran's nuclear program as the two main points of contention.

stories.title: Iran's flurry of diplomacy continues in Russia, as Trump reviews Iran's latest proposal
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia on Monday, after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, seeking to gain political leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the U.S. remain on hold.

stories.title: Russian superyacht sails through Strait of Hormuz despite blockade
stories.description: The 141m-long vessel, linked to a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin, cleared the waterway despite an ongoing blockade.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire
stories.description: The US announced three days ago that the ceasefire was extended by three weeks after talks.

stories.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.description: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.title: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
stories.description: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

stories.title: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.description: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.title: Trump says Israel-Lebanon ceasefire to be extended by three weeks
stories.description: Envoys from the two countries met in Washington as the fragile truce was due to expire on Sunday.

stories.title: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
stories.description: Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

stories.title: US-Kuwaiti journalist held in Kuwait over social media posts acquitted, lawyers say
stories.description: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin reportedly faced charges, including spreading false information, after he re-posted images related to the Iran war.

stories.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.description: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.title: 'Israel destroyed houses, roads, even trees': People return to destruction in southern Lebanon
stories.description: Many videos document the Israeli army’s destruction of houses and infrastructure in southern Lebanon, where it is creating a buffer zone along the border. Our Observers, many of whom wanted to return home after a ceasefire was announced, describe empty villages and razed homes.

stories.title: Life in Tehran since the ceasefire: How Iranian society is evolving
stories.description: As the stalemate continues in the Strait of Hormuz and peace talks between Washington and Tehran appear stalled, life in Iran itself seems to have returned to normal since the ceasefire. Society is even undergoing a major transformation, a process that began several months ago an...

stories.title: US startups explore genome editing in human embryos, raising ethical concerns
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, April 28: Photos of starving soldiers in Ukraine make headlines. Over in the US, Donald Trump wants Jimmy Kimmel fired – again. French papers talk about the controversial Alloncle report on the neutrality and funding of public broadcasting. Also: is genome...

stories.title: Iran offers to reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts its blockade and the war ends
stories.description: Iran offered to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz if the US lifts its blockade on the country and ends the war in a proposal that would postpone discussions on the Islamic Republic's nuclear program, two regional officials said Monday. US President Donald Trump seems unli...

stories.title: Lebanon health ministry says Israeli strikes kill 4 in south despite ceasefire
stories.description: Lebanon's health ministry said Israeli strikes on the country's south on Monday killed four people including a woman and wounded 51 others, three of them children, despite a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. According to an AFP tally of health ministry figures, Israeli stri...

stories.title: Britain's King Charles meets Trump in bid to salvage ties
stories.description: Britain's King Charles III met Donald Trump at the White House Monday, kicking off a high-stakes state visit shadowed by transatlantic tensions and a new alleged attempt to assassinate the US president. Behind the warm welcome for Charles and Queen Camilla in front of the cameras...

stories.title: Middle East war live: World airlines body chief warns of possible jet fuel shortage this summer
stories.description: International Air Transport Association head Willie Walsh on Tuesday warned that there was a real risk of a jet fuel shortage this summer due to the US-Israeli war on Iran. Walsh said that some flights could be cancelled if fuel rationing were to be put into effect. Follow our li...

stories.title: How the US-Israeli war is collapsing the sanctions regime on Iran
stories.description: Circumvention mechanisms already exist and the conflict is encouraging more entities to work with them.

stories.title: Mapping the destruction: How Israel ‘wiped out’ Lebanon’s Bint Jbeil
stories.description: An Al Jazeera visual probe maps the systematic demolition of Lebanon's border towns, mirroring the devastation in Gaza.

stories.title: When will Strait of Hormuz be ‘safe’ for commercial shipping again?
stories.description: Even if the strait reopens, shipping insurance could cost 20 times more than before the war, analysts say.

stories.title: The Process of Uranium Enrichment
stories.description: The Process of Uranium Enrichment

stories.title: How Iran has changed, and how it hasn’t, in two months of war
stories.description: Common Iranians face job losses and shortages. But key institutions remain in place, with hardliners stronger now.

stories.title: Down but not out: In war with Israel, Hezbollah shows it is still powerful
stories.description: Despite setbacks, Hezbollah emerges as a key player, tied to Lebanon’s stability and US-Iran negotiations.

stories.title: Trump reviews Iranian proposal aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The White House confirms Trump met his national security advisers on Monday to discuss the plan.

stories.title: 5 sentenced to life in Bahrain for plotting 'terrorist acts' with Iran
stories.description: Five people were sentenced to life in Bahrain on Tuesday for plotting “terrorist and hostile acts” with Iran, which bombarded the tiny Gulf state during the Middle East war. Two Afghans and three Bahrainis were convicted and a fourth Bahraini was acquitted by the High Criminal Co...

stories.title: Russia backs Iran as peace efforts with US remain stalled
stories.description: • Putin says Moscow will do everything to secure peace deal• Araghchi blames ‘excessive US demands’ for failure of dialogue• White House stays mum as Trump meets security aides to discuss Tehran’s proposal• Rubio says Iran ‘serious’ about deal, cites bleak economic outlook• Iran,...

stories.title: Israel strikes Bekaa Valley in violation of ceasefire
stories.description: BEIRUT: The Israeli military began carrying out strikes in eastern Lebanon on Monday, expanding the scope of its bombing campaign during a ceasefire that has failed to fully halt hostilities with Hezbollah. The strikes on Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa Valley marked the first time the a...

stories.title: Muted traffic in Hormuz strains Gulf economies
stories.description: • Seven ships pass vital waterway in a day, compared to pre-war average of 140• Iranian lawmaker says military to manage maritime route as UN chief urges toll-free waterway• UAE rebukes Gulf allies for ‘meek’ political and military response to Tehran strikes• Germany’s Merz says ...

stories.title: Bahrain revokes citizenship of 69 people for 'glorifying or sympathising' with Iranian attacks
stories.description: Bahrain revoked the citizenship of 69 people over what it described as sympathy with Iran’s hostile acts and collaboration with foreign entities, the kingdom’s interior ministry said in a statement. The ministry said that the 69 people included accused individuals and their famil...

stories.title: Iran FM Araghchi says he discussed conditions for continuing US talks with ‘friends in Pakistan’
stories.description: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Monday said he discussed with “friends in Pakistan” the “path and conditions” under which Tehran could continue its dialogue with Washington during his recent visit to Islamabad. After visiting Pakistan twice in two days and making a trip...

stories.title: U.S. says examining latest Iran proposal on Hormuz
stories.description: Iran has blockaded the waterway — a vital conduit for oil and gas shipments — since the start of the U.S.-Israeli offensive two months ago, sending shockwaves through the global economy

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE: U.S. says examining latest Iran proposal on Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: U.S., Iran clash over Tehran's nuclear programme as review of atomic treaty begins at UN; Iranian Foreign Minister praises Russia ties, welcomes Moscow's support for diplomacy

stories.title: U.S. no longer in position to ‘dictate’ policy to other nations, says Iran
stories.description: While a ceasefire has halted the fighting between Iran, the U.S. and Israel, talks on bringing a permanent conclusion to the conflict have been inconclusive

stories.title: German Chancellor Merz says Iran is humiliating U.S. as talks stall
stories.description: “The Iranians are obviously very skilled at negotiating, ⁠or rather, very skilful at not negotiating, letting the Americans travel to Islamabad and then leave again without any result,” says ​German Chancellor

stories.title: Trump not happy with latest Iran proposal to end the war, U.S. official says
stories.description: With the warring sides still seemingly far ​apart, oil prices resumed their upward march, extending gains in early Asia trade on Tuesday (April 28, 2026)

stories.title: Legal limits on U.S. war involvement
stories.description: The War Powers Act requires the President to seek authorisation from Congress for the continued use of military force abroad beyond 60 days of such involvement, raising questions over the Trump administration’s future course of action in the ongoing military engagement in West As...
